summary.intent: Address a fifth round of Codex automated code review feedback on PR #675 in the fogodev/ars-ui repo, resolving review threads about checkbox/radio group validation and item registration ordering.
summary.outcome: All three Codex review threads were addressed: checkbox hidden-input configs now support unchecked required validation, radio hidden inputs dropped aria-hidden, and a new SetItems/on_items_changed API was added to RadioGroup for authoritative rendered-order synchronization. All tests, snapshots, clippy, and conformance checks passed; commit pushed.

repo: The project uses cargo-insta for snapshot testing; intentional UI/accessibility changes require running `cargo insta accept` to refresh snapshots before tests pass.
Codex reviews are triggered by posting a comment to the PR issue and polled via GitHub reactions (eyes emoji) and review thread count via GraphQL API.
The ars-components crate has a spec conformance test suite (`cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance`) and a spec validation xtask (`cargo xtask spec validate`) that must both pass.

RadioGroup and CheckboxGroup use a `RegisterItem` event for per-item mount/prop-update, but this cannot distinguish in-place prop update from DOM reorder — leading to the need for a separate `SetItems` event.
The project requires nightly rustfmt (`cargo +nightly fmt --all`) and enforces it in CI as a Formatting check.

code.finding: RegisterItem(Radio) event is used for both initial registration and prop updates; it cannot distinguish reorder from in-place update. New SetItems(Vec<Radio>) event added for authoritative rendered-order sync.
HiddenInputConfig structs needed a `checked` field to represent unchecked required validation state for native form validation support.
Radio hidden inputs were incorrectly marked aria-hidden; this was removed since labels now target the hidden input directly.

workflow: The Codex review loop requires explicit polling with sleep intervals (90-150s) between CI check queries; no webhook-based notification is available in this workflow.

When two consecutive Codex review passes give contradictory instructions (preserve in-place vs. move to latest position), the right resolution is to redesign the API surface rather than choose one interpretation.
Staging specific files by name (`git add <explicit files>`) rather than `git add -A` is used to avoid accidentally including unintended changes.
Codex co-author trailer (`Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>`) is required on commits responding to Codex review.

friction: Codex review passes gave contradictory instructions across rounds (pass 4: preserve in-place registration; pass 5: move to latest registration position), requiring user escalation to resolve the conflict.
Multiple sleep-based polling loops (sleep 90, 120, 150 seconds) were needed to wait for CI checks and Codex reaction updates, making the review loop slow and repetitive.

Several apply_patch and write_stdin operations were needed across multiple files for what were conceptually two small fixes, indicating the hidden-input config change was spread across many call sites.
open_items: The new SetItems/on_items_changed API for RadioGroup ordered sync needs adapter implementations in Leptos and Dioxus — specs are updated but adapter code changes are not mentioned as complete.
CheckboxGroup may need a similar SetItems ordered-sync event if keyed reorder is also a concern there (only RadioGroup received the new event in this session).
The registration-order conflict pattern (RegisterItem overloaded for both mount and reorder) may exist in other component machines beyond RadioGroup and CheckboxGroup.
